おはようございます、若葉です🌿

ここ数日は暑い日が続きましたが、みなさんはいかが過ごされましたでしょうか？

今日からはお天気も下降気味のようで、今日から3日間は暑さも和らぐようでひと安心ですね
寒さには強いまりもんも暑さはてっきりなので毎年へばっていますが...

そして今日1909年は味の素の発売日。料理大好きなまりもんは化学調味料を一切使いませんが（笑）

さて、好評の明日はイラコン締切！　ステキな作品をお待ちしていますね！

本日より『うさぎと狼』『らいだ〜く』のイラストを公開していくのでお楽しみに
まりもんも鋭意製作中、ご期待ください！

今日も心地よい一日を✨
＃めびうすワークス

record.text: personally I think it's a very big deal that the Canada bureau chief for the @nytimes.com — certainly one of the highest-paid journalists in the country — asked an unspecified "AI tool" what Poilievre said & published its AI-hallucinated quotes in her reporting.

images.alt:
Editors’ Note: May 1, 2026
This article was updated after The Times learned that a remark attributed to Pierre Poilievre, the Conservative leader, was in fact an A.I.-generated summary of his views about Canadian politics that A.I. rendered as a quotation. The reporter should have checked the accuracy of what the A.I. tool returned.  The article now accurately quotes from a speech delivered by Mr. Poilievre in April. He said, “My personal opinion is that when a member of Parliament goes back on the word they made to their constituents and switches parties, constituents should be able to petition to throw them out and have a byelection. That would put the people back in charge of our democracy rather than having dirty backroom Liberal deals by Mark Carney determine our destiny.” He did not refer to politicians who changed allegiances as turncoats in that speech.

record.text:
The word generative is sixty-one years old. Max Bense coined it on 5 February 1965 in Stuttgart, in an essay describing how programs could compute aesthetics. 
I guess Bense didn't claim: "this changes everything..."
#GenerativeAI #AI

Everyone talks about Generative UI like it was invented in 2026.

Muriel Cooper built Information Landscapes at MIT in 1985: three-dimensional, dynamic typography you could fly through. She called it a "new medium" before anyone took interface seriously. Four decades early.

Star Trek, 1987. Actress terrified of the bridge panels. Designer: "Every button you press is the right button. The system adapts to you." Adaptive interfaces, 30 years before your phone learnt how you type. Read more on my Substack:

h1manu.substack.com/p/what-jony-ive-borrowed-from-star

LLM 推理現在是個熱門話題。想讓 LLM 表現更好，通常會往模型大小、訓練資料下手，但這篇文章探討的是 inference 階段的技術，尤其針對 reasoning 能力。目前看下來，透過修改 prompting 方式來提升 reasoning 能力，效果普通，而且 scaling 比較受限。相反的，如果能夠在 inference 階段引入更多運算，例如用 agent-like 的機制，讓模型有更多思考時間或步驟，會是未來提升推理能力的一個方向。這部分實測起來效果比較好，也更具擴展性。

#LLM #Inference #Reasoning #Agent

https://magazine.

Gemma 2 發布了。這篇文章分析了 Gemma 1 的表現，並討論開源模型要成功，關鍵點不會只是 benchmark 分數。

目前看下來，許多開源模型雖然在基準測試上數字不錯，但實際應用時，常常在複雜指令理解、即時反應或特定領域的細緻度上遇到瓶頸。這點跟過去只看 benchmark 差異不大。

文章提到，一個模型能被廣泛採用，除了性能，更在於社群是否能有效利用它來開發新功能或解決特定問題。這可能包含模型的彈性、客製化能力，以及與現有工具整合的容易程度。這些「軟實力」反而對模型的長期發展更重要。

#Gemma2 #開源模型 #LLM

https://www.interconnects

本週的開源進展，看到越來越多新組織投入。Nvidia 發表了 Nemotron-4 340B，一套包含訓練資料、模型權重、以及工具的開源模型，主打企業應用。印度公司 Sarvam AI 則推出開源的 IndicTransV2 語音翻譯模型，支援多種印度語言。Cohere 也發布了 Transcribe，一個支援 100 種語言的語音轉文字模型。這些進展顯示開源模型生態系持續擴大，並且朝向更多元、更專業化的方向發展，對特定應用場景會有不錯的助益。

#LLM #開源 #語音辨識 #機器學習
